architecture.summary: GASE integrates frozen LLM embeddings with graph structure for text-attributed graphs through two stages: (1) Training-Free Structure-Aware Semantic Extraction (TSSE) uses inter-layer semantic feedback and progressive masked attention to compress and propagate semantic context from neighboring nodes without updating LLM parameters; (2) Subspace Decomposition and Structural Injection (SDSI) decomposes embeddings into semantic-rich and structural-injection subspaces, injecting graph signals into the latter while preserving original LLM semantics. No LLM parameters are trained.
trained_components: Subspace Decomposition and Structural Injection projector

biblio.title: GASE: Graph-Aware Semantic Embedding Learning with Frozen LLMs for Text-Attributed Graphs

claims.statement: GASE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on node classification
GASE achieves a 5× speedup over fine-tuning-based methods
TSSE efficiently compresses and propagates semantic context from neighboring nodes without updating LLM parameters
SDSI preserves original semantics while integrating graph information through decomposition into semantic-rich subspace and structural injection subspace
Embeddings obtained from frozen LLMs lack topology awareness
Naively reducing dimensionality of LLM embeddings tends to destroy semantics
